steps.title: Identify what each coordinate means
steps.content: In this scatterplot, the $x$-coordinate is the wolf's weight in pounds and the $y$-coordinate is the number of offspring. The question gives the weight, $50$ pounds, so we need the data point whose $x$-coordinate is $50$.

steps.title: Read the point at x equals 50
steps.content: Find $50$ on the horizontal axis and move straight up to the plotted dot. That dot is at $(50,6)$, so the $50$-pound gray wolf produced $6$ offspring.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A, $8$, belongs to the heaviest wolf near $66$ pounds. Choice B, $7$, belongs to wolves heavier than $50$ pounds. Choice D, $5$, belongs to lighter wolves around $40$ to $43$ pounds. The $50$-pound wolf has $6$ offspring, so C is correct.
